title: immaturely intense roasted korean oysters bowl
description: roasted, crunchy, sick-day korean dish featuring oysters, shio-cure, flexitarian-friendly, served in a dolsot stone bowl
instructions: in a large bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, ginger, and garlic.
add the oysters to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
place the oysters on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
roast the oysters in the preheated oven for 8-10 minutes, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
while the oysters are roasting, heat the sesame oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
add the shio-cured daikon to the saucepan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it is slightly caramelized.
fluff the cooked rice with a fork and divide it among four dolsot stone bowls.
top each bowl with a roasted oyster, a few slices of shio-cured daikon, and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
garnish with chopped green onions and serve immediately.
